title: Let the grab-navigation handoff fully yield controller-motion movement
summary: 0.6.31 keeps the current button 5 grab-navigation handoff seam, but stops setting VaM's global navigation-disabled bit during that handoff. The goal is to let accel and gyro grab-drag motion fully engage even while the user is still gazing at the screen.
changes: Keep the dedicated grab-navigation handoff state introduced in 0.6.30.
During the handoff, leave VaM's global disableNavigation bit off so controller-motion grab-nav can still move the user.
Still keep ordinary internal navigation bindings disabled during the handoff and keep local CUA navigation handling out of the way.
Restore the original VaM navigation snapshot cleanly when the handoff ends.
reasoning: 0.6.30 stopped broad stick locomotion from leaking through, but the operator reported that grab-nav only almost engaged and still felt partially captured.
Official VaM grab-navigation is a distinct path from ordinary locomotion, so the handoff should avoid disabling more of VaM navigation than necessary.
This slice stays in the same CUA input seam and does not reopen playback, scene, playlist, or surface work.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: While holding button 5 and still gazing at the screen, controller-motion grab-nav should fully move and reposition the user instead of feeling partially blocked.
The player should still stay out of the way during the handoff and only re-engage focus through the existing gaze and stop rules after release.
knownIssues: This slice does not add the queued post-navigation deadband yet.
This slice does not change slideshow, A-B policy, volume gestures, or spatial audio.
